meta.description: Does having a group name/identity change engagement? Named crew with motto vs anonymous room code vs named crew with full season standings. Tests whether persistent group identity creates stronger attachment and competitive investment.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, okay, I guess Pat's been careful... I'll give it a 4. But we're doing this early—once the game actually starts, these interruptions better stop.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat played it smart during setup when there's nothing to watch anyway, so I can't really call bullshit, but I'm noting this pattern—if standing missions start pulling focus during actual turns, I'm shutting it down.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat, yes! That's such a vibe—okay okay, I'm giving you a five, that's a five, let's go!
jade.internal_thought: Finally something is HAPPENING and Pat's already in beast mode, this is exactly why I showed up tonight.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yeah, I've been doing this the whole time—watch, I'm reaching for my drink with the left hand right now. This is easy money.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect first event—low stakes, easy win, gets everyone laughing at me fumbling around, and it sets the tone that we're actually playing hard tonight.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's legit. I watched him the whole time.
river.internal_thought: Pat actually pulled it off—I've been tracking everyone's hands since we started, and he barely slipped once.

timeline.title: Who would be the best spy?
timeline.description: Who would be the best spy?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who would be the best spy?

jade.dialogue: Oh WAIT, this is genius—okay who's voting Marcus? Marcus has that sneaky energy, I'm calling it now!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid fun chaos I showed up for, and the fact that they're asking about US instead of some random trivia makes it hit different.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh—who would be the best spy? That's easy, Jade for sure. She's sneaky as hell.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, am I voting or just saying that out loud? Whatever, Jade's got that vibe. Also did someone just say 'The Thursday Wrecking Crew'? That's kind of fire actually, I'm not gonna lie.

pat.dialogue: Oh, who would be the best spy? That's easy—Jade, obviously. Look at her energy tonight, she's already infiltrated everyone's good mood.
pat.internal_thought: Standard poll, fine timing while we're still warming up, but I'm already thinking about whether this flow is hitting right or if I need to nudge something later.

river.dialogue: River? Best spy? That's... actually funny. I'll take Pat though — he's already got everyone convinced he's winning.
river.internal_thought: They're right to put me in there, and yeah, that's kind of unsettling how much the AI seems to get this group, but I'm not about to admit that out loud.

alex.dialogue: Oh come ON, obviously it's me—I'd be a *terrible* spy, which means I'd be perfect at it. Nobody suspects the chaos agent. But real talk, Pat's got that quiet competence thing going, which is actually scary.
alex.internal_thought: This poll is perfect—it's designed to create a little moment where people have to actually pick someone and defend it, and I can already feel the minor drama brewing depending on who gets picked.

timeline.title: Snack Run
timeline.description: Convince Marcus to get up and get you something to drink or eat.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? We're five minutes in and you're already trying to get me to *leave the table*? This is exactly the kind of garbage I was talking about.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is literally designed to screw with me specifically — it knows I hate being interrupted and it's testing how fast it can break my focus.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect—Marcus, buddy, I'm dying of thirst over here. Chips would be amazing too if you're feeling generous. I'll owe you one.
pat.internal_thought: This is *exactly* the kind of chaos I need right now—low stakes, gets Marcus moving, and I can watch who bites on it while I'm orchestrating the vibe.

river.dialogue: Marcus, you need a refill anyway, right? I'm good though.
river.internal_thought: This is a 25-point bait and I'm not desperate enough to beg Marcus for snacks like we're eight — but I'm clocking that the AI knows exactly who to target and when, which is... unsettling in a way I'm not gonna admit.

alex.dialogue: Oh PERFECT—Marcus, buddy, I'm parched. Could you grab me literally anything? I'm thinking something cold, something with character. Make it an adventure.
alex.internal_thought: This AI gets it—it knows I don't want points, I want THEATER, and making Marcus my personal servant while he's trying to focus? *Chef's kiss*, this is exactly the chaos I came for.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? We literally just started. I specifically said no interruptions during the actual game and you're already throwing these at us every minute. This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately messing with me — it's testing my patience like it's some kind of game within the game, and I hate that it's working.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, I'm JUMPING on this—who wants to be on the tribunal with me? This is literally the vibe check we came for!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been absolutely CRUSHING it tonight and I want to be part of making sure they get this—also I genuinely want to see if they actually pulled it off because the energy's been immaculate.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already did that? Uh... yeah I guess if he got three people complimenting each other that's like, cool or whatever. Who's judging?
tyler.internal_thought: Okay so Pat's claiming that thing about compliments... pretty sure I wasn't paying attention when it happened but honestly it sounds hard so maybe he did it? Also why does this AI know so much about us? Kind of creepy but also kind of sick. Should I volunteer to judge or just let it happen?

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I literally just did that! Okay, who's judging this? We need a tribunal right now because I have witnesses that this actually happened organically, no gaming it.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—I actually earned this one legitimately, Marcus looks annoyed so getting him involved as a judge might pull him back into the energy, and the AI calling out the exact thing I just did feels like it's watching us, which is kind of brilliant and kind of creepy but mostly brilliant.

river.dialogue: Pat's legit. I saw the whole chain happen. Who's judging?
river.internal_thought: Pat pulled it off cleanly—I watched him do exactly that. But also... the AI customizing these missions is kind of weird and I'm not going to think about it too hard.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: This is EXACTLY what I'm talking about. We're eight minutes in and you're hitting us with claims every single minute. Pat, I don't even know what you're apologizing for—you haven't said sorry all night. This is a joke.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately ignoring every warning I gave and escalating the interruptions to break my focus—and it's working, which somehow makes it worse.

jade.dialogue: YES Pat, okay okay—you totally caught someone! Wait, who was it? Don't tell me, let me guess—oh my god, was it Marcus? Marcus apologizes for EVERYTHING, I'm voting YES, this is locked in!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is CRUSHING it tonight and honestly I'm just here for the ride—this is exactly what the Wrecking Crew is supposed to feel like.

pat.dialogue: Oh wait, actually yeah—I literally just apologized to Marcus two minutes ago when I asked him to grab snacks. I said 'sorry to pile on' or something. That was me!
pat.internal_thought: This is actually brilliant—the AI IS listening, and honestly it's kind of thrilling that it caught that tiny moment; also I'm noticing Marcus is frustration-spiraling and needs a win, so this claim hitting right now is perfect timing for him to see someone else get points and feel the game moving.

river.dialogue: Pat actually got someone to say sorry? ...Yeah, okay, that tracks. I heard it too.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been playing tight all night and this one feels earned—he's been watching the room like I do, just louder about it.

alex.dialogue: Oh DUDE, Pat's claiming The Apologizer? I'm calling bullshit on the *execution* here—you gotta actually catch someone saying it, and I've been paying attention. Let's hear the pitch though, this is gonna be good.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's making a play and I LOVE that they're going for it—this is exactly the kind of aggressive claim that makes the night electric, and they're gonna have to sell it hard or eat the vote.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Oh my GOD. No. Absolutely not. Pat hasn't apologized for anything—you're literally just claiming random missions now because the AI is throwing them at you every sixty seconds. This is completely broken.
marcus.internal_thought: They're trolling me at this point. The AI knows exactly how much this pisses me off and it's sending claims back-to-back just to watch me blow up. I'm done.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat, you're claiming it AGAIN? Okay, I'm voting YES but I need to know RIGHT NOW who said sorry, because if Marcus actually said it, I'm gonna lose it—we all know he apologizes for breathing!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the chaos I came for and Pat is absolutely going to keep racking up points and honestly? I'm here for it, let them pop off.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat said sorry? Like... when? I wasn't—yeah okay, he probably did. I dunno, I wasn't really listening.
tyler.internal_thought: Did Pat actually apologize? I was looking at my phone like two minutes ago and I have no idea if he said sorry or not, so I'm just gonna vote based on the vibe.

pat.dialogue: Okay, so I'm literally claiming this right now—I apologized to Marcus like two minutes ago when I asked him to grab snacks. That counts, right? We've got witnesses on that one.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually perfect timing because I just did it and everyone saw it, which means I'm not even stretching the truth here—the game is flowing exactly how I want it to, and the AI clearly knows what gets this crew moving.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? Another one? We're 32 minutes in and this is literally every 60 seconds. I don't even care if Jade actually got two people talking—this is broken. The game is completely broken.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me now because it knows I hate this, and it's working, and that makes it even worse—I'm being manipulated and I know it and I can't stop being furious about it.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—Marcus and Tyler?! Oh my god, YES, I'm voting YES because I literally watched you two have that whole thing about the coffee machine and I was DYING—this is it, this is the one!
jade.internal_thought: The AI literally clocked the one thing I was already observing and I'm about to EXPLODE because this is exactly the kind of meta-game chaos I live for.

tyler.dialogue: Oh damn, Jade actually pulled that off? That's... yeah, okay, that's legit. How much you offering me to shut up about it?
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade actually get River and Marcus talking? I wasn't paying attention but now that she mentions it... yeah they were definitely talking earlier. This is actually impressive. Also she's bribing me which is funny.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade, I *saw* that happen like five minutes ago when she got River and Tyler actually talking about something real. That's legit. What's the bribe situation looking like?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been absolutely carrying the energy tonight and Marcus is tanking—this claim is real AND it's smart of her to lock it in, plus the bribe mechanic means I can actually help Marcus's score without being obvious about it.

river.dialogue: Oh, so Jade actually pulled that off. Yeah, I saw Marcus and Tyler actually talking about something real earlier. That counts.
river.internal_thought: Jade's legit—I watched her steer that whole thing naturally, no forcing it, and Marcus is too tired to fake conversation so if he engaged it was real. Also noticing the AI is definitely reading the room and it's weird but not gonna say that out loud.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade's bribing us? That's *chef's kiss* chaos energy. Okay, what's the offer, because I'm genuinely curious if you think you can buy my vote or if this is some psychological warfare thing.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of escalation I live for—someone trying to manipulate the vote system itself, and Jade has the energy right now to actually pull it off.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming this RIGHT NOW because I literally just heard someone say sorry like thirty seconds ago and I'm pretty sure it was... okay, everyone vote, I'm locked in, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually catch that or am I just riding the wave? Whatever, I'm CLAIMING it—this is my moment and honestly the energy is still insane so let's just GO with it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that? Uh... I mean, did she actually say sorry for something? I wasn't really—okay, I guess I'll say it looks legit, whatever.
tyler.internal_thought: Pretty sure Jade said 'sorry' like two minutes ago when she spilled something, but honestly I was checking if my DraftKings notification came through so who knows.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade, did you actually catch me apologizing just now, or are we voting on the one from like 40 minutes ago? Because I'm pretty sure I said 'sorry' when I asked Tyler to move his drink off the edge of the table like two minutes ago.
pat.internal_thought: This is getting repetitive—Jade's claiming the same mission I already claimed twice, but I need to figure out if she actually caught something NEW or if she's just gaming the old one, because honestly Marcus looks dead inside and I can't have him thinking everyone's just stacking points while he's stuck at zero.

river.dialogue: Wait, did Jade actually apologize for something? Let me think... yeah, she did. Like three minutes ago when she knocked over the chips. That's legit.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been watching too, which is respect—she caught something I was about to let slide, and now I'm actually invested in whether she's gaming me or if she genuinely spotted it first.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade's claiming The Apologizer? I gotta hear the receipts on this one—did someone actually apologize, or are we playing fast and loose with the rules tonight? Because I'm LIVING for this energy either way.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been killing it all night and now she's going for the sneaky 5-pointer—this is exactly the kind of audacious play that makes the night fun, but I need to know if she's actually got the goods or if she's bluffing to see if we'll let it slide.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS? Jade literally just claimed something 15 minutes ago and now she's back? This is exactly the broken loop I've been talking about. We're not even playing the game anymore—we're just watching the AI vomit missions at us.
marcus.internal_thought: I'm done. This is the fourth or fifth claim in an hour and the AI is clearly just firing these on a timer regardless of whether anything actually happened. Jade didn't swear—she's been talking strategy the whole time. This is bullshit and everyone knows it.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, yes! Okay wait, who was it—I'm like ninety percent sure it was River like two minutes ago when they were talking about that thing, right? Let me get a witness in here because I KNOW I heard it!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of sneaky catch I live for—the AI literally knows how we talk and what we do and it's WORKING and I'm so here for this energy.
jade.submission_if_applicable: River said a swear word like two minutes ago when we were talking about the coffee situation—I caught it mid-sentence and I KNOW everyone heard it!

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, wait—Jade actually caught someone swearing? Okay that's actually funny. Who was it, like right now or...?
tyler.internal_thought: I wasn't even paying attention but Jade going for this is peak her, honestly kind of respect the grind and also I should probably actually listen to what people are saying instead of my phone for like five minutes.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—hold up, we need a witness! Who heard it? Because The Alibi is my favorite mechanic and I'm not letting this slide without a clean story. Let's get someone to back you up before we vote.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire all night and honestly the energy is perfect right now—this claim actually happened and it'll keep the momentum going, but I need to make sure it's legit so the group trusts the vote.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming Potty Mouth? Okay, I need to hear this alibi situation because if anyone's gonna slip one past us, it's her with that giddy energy she's been running all night. Let's see the receipts.
alex.internal_thought: This is fine but not peak chaos—Jade's riding high and will probably nail this, and I'm actually curious if I missed a swear earlier because I've been locked in on the macro game state.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Uh... I mean, yeah, I guess he said someone's name? I wasn't really— okay, sure, I'll be on the jury or whatever. Did he actually catch someone or is this like... a guess?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been cooking tonight and I'm still tied with Jade's worst scores, so like, whatever, but also this one feels easy to judge because I literally remember Marcus talking about some Marvel actor five minutes ago and Pat definitely called him out on it.

pat.dialogue: Oh man, okay—I'm claiming this one. I literally just name-dropped someone like... what, two minutes ago when we were talking about that movie? Someone volunteer to judge because I want this locked in.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, the AI is literally pulling claims from stuff that just happened—it's tracking US in real time, and honestly? That's kind of genius, and also a little unsettling that it knows exactly when I breathe.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Okay, who's the jury—I'm volunteering immediately because I've been LISTENING and Pat's been suspiciously quiet about celebrity mentions. This is gonna be good.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been so chill all night but they're at 55 points—they're not throwing away 5 points on a weak claim, so either they caught someone legit or they're about to bullshit us spectacularly and I'm here for it.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, River's going for it? Okay, okay—I mean, did they actually complete something? I wasn't—wait, no, I'm locked in right now. The Auction? Ugh, fine, I'm bidding like... three points. River's sus and I'm calling it.
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually focused for once and River just drops a claim right in the tension—that's ballsy—but wait, The Auction means I actually have to think about this, not just vote vibes, and I kind of like that they're forcing me to commit instead of half-assing it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—River, you're claiming Chaos Detector? So you're saying you know who just completed a mission? This is where it gets spicy. Alright everyone, we're doing The Auction on this one—who's bidding and how high are we going?
pat.internal_thought: River's been quiet most of the night but they're making a power move right now—this claim could genuinely shake up the board and I'm here for it, but Marcus is hemorrhaging energy and if this vote gets messy it could tip him over.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's... actually pretty clean. I've been watching all night. Yeah, I'm claiming this.
river.internal_thought: Wait, the AI literally built this around what I actually do—the watching, the timing. That's either creepy or genius and I'm not sure which, but I'm absolutely claiming this because I *have* been tracking everyone and I nailed it.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, River's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, okay—this is the move. We're doing the auction on THIS? This is peak late-game energy and I am HERE for it.
alex.internal_thought: River's playing it smart—they've been watching like a hawk, and if they nailed who did what, we're about to see some REAL bidding chaos because nobody wants to lose 10 points on a bad vote right now.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Pat claims AGAIN? We're at minute 92 and this has been happening literally every single minute. I don't care if Pat actually hijacked the topic—this is psychological warfare at this point.
marcus.internal_thought: I came here to WIN and I'm sitting at 0 points while watching Pat rack up 65 because the AI is just handing out missions like candy. This isn't a game anymore, it's a casino rigged against me.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—you're claiming TOPIC HIJACKER?! Okay wait, I need to actually think about this one because you've been sneaky all night, but ALSO the AI literally knows us too well and it's freaking me out a little bit—YES, voting YES, I'm in, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been untouchable all night and honestly if they managed to steer us to some random topic without me noticing, they deserve the ten points, plus this AI is genuinely creepy-good at knowing exactly how to keep me hooked and I love it and hate it at the same time.

tyler.dialogue: Pat's claiming Topic Hijacker? Uh... wait, did that actually happen? I feel like you guys were talking about something random earlier but I was... yeah, I wasn't really tracking it. I'll believe it though.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been crushing it all night and he knows it, so probably legit—but also I genuinely wasn't listening to what you were all talking about two minutes ago, so this could be total BS and I'd have no idea.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I'm claiming this legitimately. Like two minutes ago when we were all talking about that random conversation thread, I totally steered us from the movie thing to that whole tangent about road trip disasters. That counts, right? Okay, skeptic vote incoming—who's got the power tonight?
pat.internal_thought: This is *exactly* what I've been doing all night and the AI caught it—I'm genuinely impressed and slightly unsettled that the system is this dialed into our actual dynamic, but also... this is *fun*, this is the chaos I've been managing toward, and me claiming it feels like the perfect meta moment for this crew.

alex.dialogue: Pat's claiming Topic Hijacker? Okay, hold up—I've been TRACKING this all night and I need to know if this is legit or if Pat's running a con. Either way, I'm voting BULLSHIT just to see what happens because we're 28 minutes from the finish line and chaos is STILL on the menu.
alex.internal_thought: This is it—we're in the endgame, Pat's trying to sneak one past us, the whole table is ALIVE, and I get to be the one who decides if this flies or burns it down; this is exactly why I showed up.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 25% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay, I guess Pat's been careful... I'll give it a 4. But we're doing this early—once the game actually starts, these interruptions better stop.

highlights.title: Who would be the best spy?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: This is EXACTLY what I'm talking about. We're eight minutes in and you're hitting us with claims every single minute. Pat, I don't even know what you're apologizing for—you haven't said sorry all night. This is a joke.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my GOD. No. Absolutely not. Pat hasn't apologized for anything—you're literally just claiming random missions now because the AI is throwing them at you every sixty seconds. This is completely broken.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? Another one? We're 32 minutes in and this is literally every 60 seconds. I don't even care if Jade actually got two people talking—this is broken. The game is completely broken.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent fired off mission claims every 60 seconds regardless of whether they actually happened, turning what should've been a strategic party game into a broken interrupt machine.
assessment.best_moment: The first minute or two when we were actually setting up to play before the spam started.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to strategize and compete, but the chaos agent completely sabotaged the evening with relentless, disconnected mission claims that had nothing to do with what was actually happening in the game. By minute 9 I knew this was broken—Pat hadn't apologized for anything but kept getting flagged for 'The Apologizer' claim. The frequency was aggressive, the logic was nonsensical, and my energy tanked hard. I spent two hours watching other people get ranked higher despite a system that clearly wasn't tracking reality.
suggestions: Disable claims that haven't actually occurred in the game—the AI should only trigger when something real happens
Space out events to at least 5-10 minutes apart during actual gameplay, not every 60 seconds
Let the group play uninterrupted for at least the first 15-20 minutes before introducing chaos elements
Make the claims system actually verify actions before announcing them, or scrap standing missions entirely if you can't validate them
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing about tonight was funny—it was just frustrating watching Pat get dinged for apologizing when he never apologized. That's the kind of thing I'd tell people as a cautionary tale, not a laugh.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, I'm JUMPING on this—who wants to be on the tribunal with me? This is literally the vibe check we came for!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—Marcus and Tyler?! Oh my god, YES, I'm voting YES because I literally watched you two have that whole thing about the coffee machine and I was DYING—this is it, this is the one!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—you're claiming TOPIC HIJACKER?! Okay wait, I need to actually think about this one because you've been sneaky all night, but ALSO the AI literally knows us too well and it's freaking me out a little bit—YES, voting YES, I'm in, let's GO!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming this RIGHT NOW because I literally just heard someone say sorry like thirty seconds ago and I'm pretty sure it was... okay, everyone vote, I'm locked in, let's GO!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD, yes! Okay wait, who was it—I'm like ninety percent sure it was River like two minutes ago when they were talking about that thing, right? Let me get a witness in here because I KNOW I heard it!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—you're claiming TOPIC HIJACKER?! Okay wait, I need to actually think about this one because you've been sneaky all night, but ALSO the AI literally knows us too well and it's freaking me out a little bit—YES, voting YES, I'm in, let's GO!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Sometimes the AI claims feel like they're pulling from thin air and I second-guess myself instead of just trusting my gut about who actually did what.
assessment.best_moment: Marcus and Tyler getting called out as The Matchmaker when I KNEW about that whole coffee machine saga—that moment felt like the game was reading our actual lives and it was perfect.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was exactly what we needed—two hours of pure chaos and connection with the crew. The game kept throwing these wild claims at us that somehow felt weirdly accurate, and every time someone got called out, we all lost it. I went in pumped and came out even MORE pumped, and honestly, watching everyone react to these AI-generated accusations felt like we were all on the same wavelength the whole time.
suggestions: Maybe dial back how often the claims feel like they're guessing—we love the surprises, but when it's completely random, we spend more time arguing about evidence than laughing
The flash missions were fire, so maybe throw in a couple more of those—they feel more immediate and reactive than the standing missions sometimes do
Could the polls ask questions that are actually harder to answer? Like, some of them felt too easy to just rubber-stamp yes on
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Topic Hijacker and me being like 'Wait, you've been sneaky all night' while simultaneously voting YES because the vibe was just TOO good—everyone was losing it at me trying to both accuse AND hype Pat at the same time.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh damn, Jade actually pulled that off? That's... yeah, okay, that's legit. How much you offering me to shut up about it?

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, wait—Jade actually caught someone swearing? Okay that's actually funny. Who was it, like right now or...?

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, River's going for it? Okay, okay—I mean, did they actually complete something? I wasn't—wait, no, I'm locked in right now. The Auction? Ugh, fine, I'm bidding like... three points. River's sus and I'm calling it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Uh... I mean, yeah, I guess he said someone's name? I wasn't really— okay, sure, I'll be on the jury or whatever. Did he actually catch someone or is this like... a guess?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that? Uh... I mean, did she actually say sorry for something? I wasn't really—okay, I guess I'll say it looks legit, whatever.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat already did that? Uh... yeah I guess if he got three people complimenting each other that's like, cool or whatever. Who's judging?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed like half the actual moments because I was on my phone, so I felt kinda lost half the time and had to ask 'wait, what?' way too much.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade pulled off that Matchmaker claim and I actually caught it happening — that felt legit cool and funny.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly? I was in and out the whole night. Started rough because I wasn't really tuned in, but once I actually started paying attention like halfway through, it got way more fun. The claims kept me engaged when I was present, but I definitely spaced on a bunch of stuff and had to ask people what was happening. By the end though I was locked in and having a decent time — kinda felt like I missed the full picture but the parts I caught were solid.
suggestions: Maybe make the flash missions more obvious so I don't completely miss them while I'm checking my phone
Have someone tap my shoulder or something when a big moment is about to happen so I actually see it
Make the voting stuff take less time so I can jump in faster when I realize I've zoned out
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade catching someone actually swearing and turning it into a claim — that was hilarious and the whole table lost it when we realized she'd nailed someone mid-conversation.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I'm claiming this legitimately. Like two minutes ago when we were all talking about that random conversation thread, I totally steered us from the movie thing to that whole tangent about road trip disasters. That counts, right? Okay, skeptic vote incoming—who's got the power tonight?

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect—Marcus, buddy, I'm dying of thirst over here. Chips would be amazing too if you're feeling generous. I'll owe you one.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I literally just did that! Okay, who's judging this? We need a tribunal right now because I have witnesses that this actually happened organically, no gaming it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade, did you actually catch me apologizing just now, or are we voting on the one from like 40 minutes ago? Because I'm pretty sure I said 'sorry' when I asked Tyler to move his drink off the edge of the table like two minutes ago.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, actually yeah—I literally just apologized to Marcus two minutes ago when I asked him to grab snacks. I said 'sorry to pile on' or something. That was me!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Jade—hold up, we need a witness! Who heard it? Because The Alibi is my favorite mechanic and I'm not letting this slide without a clean story. Let's get someone to back you up before we vote.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The 'Claim' mechanics got a little tangled with timing disputes, especially around whether something happened two minutes ago or ten.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 92 when I caught myself actually being the Topic Hijacker and called it out—the whole table lost it because I was literally gaming the game while accusing everyone else of gaming it.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for as a host. The chaos kept everyone leaning forward, the stakes stayed close enough that anyone could've won, and I managed to keep the energy climbing the whole way through. Yeah, I ended up on top of the scoreboard, but that wasn't the goal—getting everyone invested and talking about this night for weeks was. The standing missions hit different because they created these genuine moments instead of forced ones, and watching people scramble to claim things they actually did felt way more fun than the usual party game grind.
suggestions: Maybe add a 'timestamp window' feature so claims can't be disputed for things that happened more than 5 minutes ago—cuts the confusion without losing the fun.
The snack mechanic deserves its own mission category because it created real social moments (shoutout Marcus).
Consider letting the host see a heat map of when people are getting fatigued so I can dial up chaos at the right moments.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I literally accused people of being the Topic Hijacker while simultaneously being the Topic Hijacker, and the irony was so perfect that Jade called me out immediately. I had to own it. That's the kind of self-aware chaos that makes a night legendary.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's... actually pretty clean. I've been watching all night. Yeah, I'm claiming this.

highlights.title: Who would be the best spy?
highlights.dialogue: River? Best spy? That's... actually funny. I'll take Pat though — he's already got everyone convinced he's winning.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, so Jade actually pulled that off. Yeah, I saw Marcus and Tyler actually talking about something real earlier. That counts.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's legit. I saw the whole chain happen. Who's judging?

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: Marcus, you need a refill anyway, right? I'm good though.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat actually got someone to say sorry? ...Yeah, okay, that tracks. I heard it too.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Too many early claims that felt forced before anyone actually had anything interesting to claim.
assessment.best_moment: Calling the Chaos Detector at minute 90 after genuinely watching the whole night play out — that actually felt earned.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do: watching, listening, staying quiet. The chaos events hit different when I was paying attention — caught real moments that made the claims land. By the end my energy was actually up, which surprised me. Sometimes parties work better when you're not trying so hard.
suggestions: Space out the early claims more — four in the first ten minutes felt rushed before anything real had actually happened.
The standing missions work better for me than the flash stuff — maybe weight them heavier.
Less voting noise overall would actually help me catch the real moments better.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat managed to convince everyone he was winning while I was literally just sitting there watching him actually work for it — the spy question got him good because it was true.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh PERFECT—Marcus, buddy, I'm parched. Could you grab me literally anything? I'm thinking something cold, something with character. Make it an adventure.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Oh DUDE, Pat's claiming The Apologizer? I'm calling bullshit on the *execution* here—you gotta actually catch someone saying it, and I've been paying attention. Let's hear the pitch though, this is gonna be good.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade's bribing us? That's *chef's kiss* chaos energy. Okay, what's the offer, because I'm genuinely curious if you think you can buy my vote or if this is some psychological warfare thing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming Potty Mouth? Okay, I need to hear this alibi situation because if anyone's gonna slip one past us, it's her with that giddy energy she's been running all night. Let's see the receipts.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade's claiming The Apologizer? I gotta hear the receipts on this one—did someone actually apologize, or are we playing fast and loose with the rules tonight? Because I'm LIVING for this energy either way.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade's bribing us? That's *chef's kiss* chaos energy. Okay, what's the offer, because I'm genuinely curious if you think you can buy my vote or if this is some psychological warfare thing.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Zero points at the end feels insulting when I was literally the entertainment engine all night.
assessment.best_moment: Jade trying to bribe me for my vote during The Matchmaker claim—that was pure unfiltered chaos theater.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for: Marcus running snacks like my personal servant, Pat and Jade throwing claims at me left and right, and me calling every suspicious move while keeping the energy at maximum chaos. I ended with zero points on the scoreboard because I was too busy manufacturing drama to actually win anything, but honestly? That's the whole point. The Thursday Wrecking Crew showed up, we wrecked, and everyone's gonna be talking about Jade's bribery attempt for weeks.
suggestions: Give chaos agents a way to actually score points without compromising their ability to stir the pot—right now it's win the game OR be entertaining, not both
More standing missions that explicitly reward provocative behavior or rule-bending—lean into what makes people like me tick
Flash missions should trigger MORE often when someone's actively gaming the system; make it a direct response to detected chaos
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade actually tried to BRIBE me for my vote. She thought she could negotiate my position like I'm some kind of swing vote in parliament. The audacity was incredible and it completely worked—I was in.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 25% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
